title: Chrysodium aureonitens
intro: Chrysodium aureonitens is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the golden maidenhair fern. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysodium aureonitens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and joint pain.
taxonomy: Chrysodium aureonitens is a type of fern, which falls under the classification of the plant kingdom. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. More specifically, Chrysodium aureonitens can be classified as a member of the family Polypodiaceae and the genus Chrysodium. The species is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is often found in shaded areas with high humidity. Classification of plants is an ongoing process, and some sources may group Chrysodium aureonitens differently.
